requiredBehaviors.behavior: The first-run API-key step accepts pasted BYOK provider keys, verifies them against the selected provider, and asks for explicit approval before persistence.
Approved BYOK keys are persisted through LocalAuthBackend in private auth state and are read during runtime startup before managed-key vending.
Very large pasted onboarding inputs are split into secret-safe paste metadata plus private paste-cache entries stored with mode 0600 only after explicit key approval.

intentionalReductions: AgenC persists approved provider keys as provider-neutral BYOK records in LocalAuthBackend instead of keeping approved/rejected key tails in global UI config.
AgenC verifies keys with provider metadata already owned by the runtime registry; it does not execute donor-specific API-key helper commands.

Paste-cache helpers avoid secret-bearing telemetry and log output. The API-key onboarding path does not render raw large-paste previews; it renders masked tails plus paste metadata and defers disk persistence until approval.
